title: eremophila lactea
intro: Eremophila lactea, also known as Milkwood or White Bugle, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial actions. The plant is said to provide several health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Eremophila lactea are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties.
